- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

retrieved.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

retrieved.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
retrieved.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. retrieved.title: Additional degree programmes
retrieved.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

retrieved.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
retrieved.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

- [Financial support by the University of Vienna](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (in German)
- [Research Services and Career Development](https://forschung.univie.ac.at/en/)

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
You can be admitted to non-degree programmes for different reasons. In a non-degree programme

- ... you are not admitted to a specific degree programme.
- ... you can download your student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment in u:space, as soon as your [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) is received by the University of Vienna.
- ... a transcript of records will be issued for any exams you take. It is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
- ... you have to take note of several consequences.

heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at another educational institution
retrieved.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you have studied or are currently studying one of the following programmes at a university or college of teacher education in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland.

Sie sind vom Eignungsverfahren für das Bachelorstudium Lehramt an der Universität Wien ausgenommen, wenn Sie eines der folgenden Studien an einer Universität oder Pädagogischen Hochschule eines EU/EWR-Landes oder der Schweiz studieren oder abgeschlossen haben.

- Bachelor's programme in Teacher Education for secondary schools (general education)
- Programme in Business Education
- Programme in Religious Education

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. your current confirmation of studies or your degree certificate
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message. Note: If you want to study the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education please also send your confirmation of studies to [StudiesServiceCenter Sport Science](#).

retrieved.title: Entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna conducts an entrance exam or aptitude test procedure for certain bachelor's and diploma programmes. This means: a written test will take place in July 2026.

On this page, you can find out everything you need to know about the programme content, deadlines, required documents and the test procedure. We provide a step by step guide on how to prepare and how to successfully apply.

When clicking the play button personal data may be transferred to YouTube.

reranked.text:

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

reranked.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

reranked.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
reranked.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
reranked.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. reranked.title: Additional degree programmes
reranked.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

reranked.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
reranked.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
You can be admitted to non-degree programmes for different reasons. In a non-degree programme

- ... you are not admitted to a specific degree programme.
- ... you can download your student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment in u:space, as soon as your [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) is received by the University of Vienna.
- ... a transcript of records will be issued for any exams you take. It is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
reranked.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at another educational institution
reranked.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you have studied or are currently studying one of the following programmes at a university or college of teacher education in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland.

Sie sind vom Eignungsverfahren für das Bachelorstudium Lehramt an der Universität Wien ausgenommen, wenn Sie eines der folgenden Studien an einer Universität oder Pädagogischen Hochschule eines EU/EWR-Landes oder der Schweiz studieren oder abgeschlossen haben.

- Bachelor's programme in Teacher Education for secondary schools (general education)
- Programme in Business Education
- Programme in Religious Education

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. your current confirmation of studies or your degree certificate
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message. Note: If you want to study the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education please also send your confirmation of studies to [StudiesServiceCenter Sport Science](#).

reranked.title: Entrance exam procedure
reranked.text:
The University of Vienna conducts an entrance exam or aptitude test procedure for certain bachelor's and diploma programmes. This means: a written test will take place in July 2026.

On this page, you can find out everything you need to know about the programme content, deadlines, required documents and the test procedure. We provide a step by step guide on how to prepare and how to successfully apply.

When clicking the play button personal data may be transferred to YouTube.

The video has been loaded and can be found below on the page.

You can activate English subtitles in the video settings.

Previous

©

Next

Choose one of our bachelor's or diploma programmes. On the study page, you will find all the information you need about the programme's content and structure, as well as key details regarding the written test.

selected.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: B
selected.text:
/

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

selected.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
selected.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

selected.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
selected.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

selected.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
selected.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

[2] Bachelor/diploma programmes
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

[3] Degree Programmes offered at the University of Vienna
The University of Vienna is one of the most prestiguous and largest universities in Europe and offers a wide range of degree programmes. From education, social sciences, philosophy and religion, history, society, politics and arts over culture, media, mathematics, informatics and technology, natural and life sciences, psychology, health and sports tp languages, economic sciences and law.

This diversity gives students the opportunity to choose an individualised way through their studies that best suits their interests and abilities.

[4] Knowledge of Foreign Languages
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

[5] I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind? — I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme > General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

[6] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
You were admitted to a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to study a different degree programme? Here you can find detailed information to best prepare your return to the University of Vienna.
